papers.title: Detection and Prognostic Value of Circulating Tumor DNA in Patients With Colorectal Cancer
papers.access_route: Corresponding author at Aarhus University Hospital; Danish Data Protection Agency approval; institutional MTA required
papers.notes: Stages I-III CRC with matched FFPE + preoperative plasma. JAMA Oncology publication = high indexing confidence. Danish multicenter = access may require coordination across sites. Treatment-naive preoperative collection is a strong quality signal for ADC biomarker work.

papers.title: Circulating tumor DNA dynamics predict outcomes in stage III colon cancer treated with adjuvant chemotherapy
papers.access_route: Corresponding author at Fudan University; Chinese ethics committee approval; cross-border specimen transfer subject to Chinese regulations
papers.notes: n=165 stage III colon with FFPE + serial plasma. Adjuvant chemotherapy cohort — not treatment-naive. Serial plasma is a differentiating feature. Cross-border specimen transfer from China is a real access barrier; consider data-only collaboration as alternative.

papers.title: ctDNA as a biomarker for adjuvant chemotherapy in colorectal cancer: a prospective multicenter study
papers.access_route: Multi-country access: Danish PI contact + Spanish ethics approval; GDPR compliance required for EU-based cohort
papers.notes: n=168 stage III CRC with FFPE + plasma. Danish+Spanish multicenter = broader geographic representation. PMC indexed = high recall probability. GDPR-compliant access pathway required for EU institutions.

papers.title: A population-based study of PLCO colorectal cancer specimens: matched FFPE tumor and blood
papers.institution: PLCO (Prostate, Lung, Colorectal, and Ovarian) Screening Trial / NCI
papers.access_route: NCI Cancer Data Access System (CDAS) formal application; IRB approval; PLCO data sharing agreement
papers.notes: PLCO NCI tissue resource with matched FFPE + blood. Large population-based screening trial. Plasma specifically not described — blood specimens may be serum/plasma/buffy coat; verify matrix in CDAS data dictionary. NCI CDAS access is structured but slower (3-6 months typical).

notes: Four papers spanning European academic, Chinese academic, and US NCI infrastructure. Pipeline should retrieve on queries combining CRC + FFPE + plasma + matched + stage III OR ctDNA colorectal. PLCO/NCI (PMC5135604) is the most structured access pathway. Danish papers (PMC9401484 + PMID 31070691) are strong European options. Fudan paper (PMID 35636041) has the largest n but Chinese access barriers reduce sourcing probability. All four are PubMed-indexed = high recall.
